fingerprint: The dome's 'Forensic Zoom' exhibit (home page, dome SHA c8eaeb1b) borrows two globe-frame infrastructures wholesale: (i) GRACE orbital mechanics — altitude ~500 km, velocity ~7.5 km/s, ground-track ~12,150 km / 27 min — and (ii) INTERMAGNET/Dst geomagnetic-storm framework. Both are 3D-Earth quantities with no flat-disc analog. Our current detail_evidence uses the GRACE orbital numbers (argument 3) and the Dst = −383 nT label (argument from setup) as if they were ordinary physics, but every number in argument (3) requires a spherical mass and a polar orbit to even define. The dome cannot defend the chart on its own geometry without first deriving 'low Earth orbit' on a disc — which it cannot do, because there is none.
location: data/wins.json — WIN-070 detail_evidence argument (3) 'GRACE orbital mechanics rule out co-temporal comparison with a fixed Boulder station' and detail_evidence setup citing Dst = −383 nT, BOU (40.14°N, 105.24°W).

globe_values.name: GRACE orbital velocity
globe_values.source: Newton/Kepler orbital mechanics with M_Earth = 5.972e24 kg, R_Earth = 6371 km

globe_values.name: GRACE 27-min ground-track length
globe_values.derivation: 0.286 * 2*pi*(R_Earth + h) = 0.286 * 2*pi*6.871e6 m = 12088 km; alternatively v*t = 7.5 km/s * 1620 s = 12150 km (orbital, not ground-track; the more correct R/(R+h)-scaled ground figure is 11,440 km)
globe_values.source: spherical-orbit kinematics; both depend on the globe radius being a real 3D quantity

globe_values.name: Dst index baseline
globe_values.value: 4-station equatorial average: Hermanus (19°E), Kakioka (140°E), Honolulu (202°E), San Juan (294°E)
globe_values.source: Sugiura & Kamei 1991, IAGA Dst definition — a longitudinal average at the magnetic equator, presupposing spherical Earth coordinates

globe_values.name: INTERMAGNET ΔH reduction
globe_values.source: IGRF-13 is a spherical-harmonic expansion of B(r, θ, φ) to degree/order 13 around Earth's centre; every nT excursion the dome cites is computed against a spherical-harmonic baseline

dome_values.name: GRACE altitude (cited in our argument (3))
dome_values.note: No dome parameter describes a satellite altitude. The firmament is at 9,086 km. There is no orbital regime in the dome cosmology.

dome_values.name: GRACE orbital velocity (cited in our argument (3))
dome_values.note: On a disc with no point mass at finite r, there is no orbit and no v=sqrt(GM/r). The cited velocity is globe-physics imported.

dome_values.name: Ground-track length
dome_values.note: A "ground track" requires (i) a sub-satellite point projected vertically onto a curved surface, (ii) a rotating Earth, (iii) an inertial frame attached to a 3D mass. None exist on the disc.

dome_values.name: BOU (40.14°N, 105.24°W)
dome_values.note: Latitude exists as an angle relative to the spin axis of a sphere. On a polar-azimuthal projection of the dome, BOU is at a radial distance from the pole, not a "latitude." Citing the coordinates without remark concedes the WGS84 frame.

dome_values.name: Dst = −383 nT
dome_values.note: Dst is, by IAGA definition, a longitudinal average across 4 equatorial-magnetic observatories. Its meaning requires a spherical, dipole-tilt-having Earth. On a flat disc, there is no "equator" and no "longitudinal average."

match_precisions.cited_velocity_vs_keplerian: 7.5 km/s cited vs 7.62 km/s sqrt(GM/(R+h)) — agreement to within ~1.6%
match_precisions.cited_groundtrack_vs_keplerian: 12,150 km cited vs 12,088 km from 0.286 orbital fraction — agreement to within ~0.5%
match_precisions.dome_disc_radius_a_vs_pi_R: a = 20,015 km matches pi*R_Earth = 20,015.087 km to 4 ppm (cross-reference EXEC-SUMMARY, EVAL-GUIDE, SEC-2.1 fingerprints — this is the parent fingerprint; the WIN-070 orbital numbers are a separate borrowing)

why_it_matters: WIN-070's current verdict is strong on its own terms: (1) impossible unit label, (2) non-existent GRACE product name, (3) orbital-mechanics co-temporal impossibility, (4) zero processing code in repo, (5) missing Asymmetry-Index formula. But arguments (3) and the storm-setup citation of Dst = −383 nT both lean on globe quantities without flagging them as such. A dome defender reading the present verdict could say: 'You used GRACE orbital mechanics to refute me; that means you accept GRACE; so the chart's data source is admissible.' Adding the fingerprint preempts this rescue. The point is not just 'we beat the chart on its merits' but 'even the refutation forces the dome to import the globe.' Argument (3) is doing two jobs at once — empirical refutation and globe fingerprint — and we currently only credit it for one. Stating both makes the WIN's logic airtight against the 'asymmetric concession' move.
algebraic_check: v_orbit at h=500 km: sqrt(6.6743e-11 * 5.972e24 / (6.371e6 + 500e3)) = sqrt(3.986e14 / 6.871e6) = 7616.5 m/s. Round-trip ground-track in 27 min using orbital velocity: 7500 m/s * 1620 s = 12.15e6 m = 12,150 km. Using the more correct R/(R+h)-scaled ground-track velocity (v_ground = v_orbit * R/(R+h) = 7062 m/s): 7062 * 1620 = 11,440 km. Either way, the number only exists for a spherical mass with R_Earth ~6,371 km and GM ~3.986e14 m^3/s^2.
kernel_acknowledged: The dome did NOT invent GRACE — and our argument (3) does not need to deny GRACE exists. The empirical refutation stands: even if you grant the globe and grant GRACE, a single 500 km LEO satellite cannot phase-lock to a fixed mid-latitude station for 27 continuous minutes. The kernel that the dome got right (the BOU magnetometer top panel is real INTERMAGNET data) is unaffected. The fingerprint addition is layered ON TOP of the existing refutation, not in place of it. Kernel: argument (3) is empirically correct AND a globe fingerprint; we currently use only half of its rhetorical force.
suggested_text: Globe-fingerprint annotation on argument (3). Every quantity in the orbital-mechanics refutation above is a 3D-Earth quantity: '~500 km altitude' (above what mass?), '~7.5 km/s orbital velocity' (sqrt(GM_Earth / (R_Earth + 500 km)) = 7.62 km/s, within 1.6% of the cited value), and '~12,150 km of ground track in 27 minutes' (≈ 0.286 of an orbital circumference 2π(R_Earth + 500 km)). On the dome's geometry — a 20,015 km disc with a firmament at 9,086 km — there is no low Earth orbit, no orbital velocity sqrt(GM/r), and no projected ground track. The chart's data source is a satellite mission whose existence presupposes the geometry the dome denies; the chart's storm setup cites Dst = −383 nT, an index defined by a longitudinal average across four magnetic-equator observatories (Hermanus 19°E, Kakioka 140°E, Honolulu 202°E, San Juan 294°E), which is a spherical-Earth construction by definition. We use the globe's orbital numbers to refute the chart, but the refutation also exposes that the chart's evidence base is itself globe-derived. (See Section 2.1 for the parent disc-radius = πR fingerprint and KILLSHOT-SH for the spherical-harmonic infrastructure under IGRF / Dst.)

files_checked: GitHub tree listing for john09289/predictions (main, 200 entries)

scripts/download_l1a_manual.py (16,675 bytes) — generic cookie-authenticated downloader (Selenium/SFTP/cookie modes); no GRACE-specific parsing, no L1A-binary reader, no orbital-elements code, no R_Earth or GM constants
data/l1a_download_jobs.example.json — example job descriptor only, no processing
inject_ai_layer.py, monitor.py, pull_data.py — none mention GRACE / orbits / altitude
code_search.found_in: Dome repo at main contains no orbital-mechanics derivation of any kind. The 500-km altitude, 7.5 km/s velocity, and 12,150 km ground-track are quoted as if they were independent physical inputs but are obtained from textbook spherical orbital mechanics. The dome side has no code that derives or even mentions these numbers; they appear only in the static HTML home-page chart.
code_search.notes: Confirms WIN-070 argument (4) (no GRACE processing) and extends it: not only does the dome lack code to compute the residuals, it also lacks code to compute the orbital-mechanics quantities used to set up the chart. The numbers are imported wholesale from a 3D-Earth framework the dome elsewhere denies.

follow_up_suggestions:
Decider: consider whether the suggested_text annotation should be inserted as a new

Bonus: globe-fingerprint annotation. paragraph at the END of WIN-070 detail_evidence (after the existing five-argument structure), or whether it should be folded into argument (3) inline. The annotation does not change any existing claim — it adds a layered observation. Recommend appending; the five-argument structure is already tight.

Curmudgeon c2/c3: when this WIN gets a deeper review pass, audit whether the kernel-of-truth framing (BOU magnetometer panel real) accidentally implies INTERMAGNET data is dome-frame-neutral. INTERMAGNET reductions use IGRF, which is spherical-harmonic; even the kernel sits on globe infrastructure. Could be a c3 advocate-mode point if not handled here.

Consider a sibling fingerprint entry KILLSHOT-DST.json or expand KILLSHOT-SH.json to specifically call out Dst-index spherical-Earth dependence — the present finding only annotates WIN-070, but Dst recurrence across the dome site (WIN-012, WIN-058b, WIN-070) suggests a global treatment is warranted.
